article_meta.title: — News —Study Shows Pandemic Saw Rise In Firearm Injuries Among KidsBy Charlotte Pence Bond•Dec 20, 2022 DailyWire.com•F

reasoning: The model correctly identifies 'disastrous' as author-level bias (items 1 and 2 are essentially the same instance flagged twice with different labels). However, item 3 is an attribution rule violation - it flags a quoted statement from Stephanie Iantorno. The model acknowledges it's attributed but then argues the framing adopts the opinion, which is a stretch. The model also misses some potential bias instances but was instructed to be cautious.

custom_reasoning.attribution_rule_compliance: Item 3 flags a direct quote from Stephanie Iantorno inside quotation marks. The model acknowledges it is attributed but argues the article's framing around it presents opinion as fact. This is a borderline attribution violation - the text flagged is literally inside quotes. One clear violation out of three items.
custom_reasoning.false_positive_rate: Items 1 and 2 both flag 'disastrous' which is genuinely the author's loaded word choice - these are defensible but redundant. Item 3 flags quoted text which is a false positive. So 1 out of 3 items is a false positive, with the other two being essentially duplicates of one genuine finding.
custom_reasoning.false_negative_rate: The model catches the most obvious bias ('disastrous'). It could have potentially flagged other instances like 'a spike in gun injuries' (word choice - 'spike' is somewhat loaded) or structural slant in how the article frames the pandemic's effects, but the article is largely a reporting piece. The instruction to be cautious mitigates this somewhat. Missing some instances but caught the most prominent one.
custom_reasoning.bias_type_accuracy: Sensationalism/Emotionalism and Subjective Qualifying Adjectives are both defensible labels for 'disastrous' - though flagging the same word twice with different types is redundant rather than wrong. The Opinion Statements Presented as Fact label on the Iantorno quote is debatable even if we accepted the flagging.
